title: Bond spending

allocationSource.name: Measure W Rebase #19 and State Match Rebase #1

measureWProjectScopes.DS-003: Construction of carport canopy-mounted photovoltaic panels in Lot C and Lot E at Mission College and Lots 1, 2, and 7 at West Valley College. Scope includes construction of canopies, installation of PV panels, battery storage, and electrical infrastructure required.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-006: Utility and electrical infrastructure upgrades to buildings across both Mission College and West Valley College to improve reliability, capacity, and operational efficiency. Scope includes infrastructure assessment.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-007: Upgrade underground water and sewer infrastructure across Mission College and West Valley College to improve reliability, capacity, and system performance. Scope includes infrastructure assessment.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-008: Expand ADA parking to improve accessibility and safety to the campus. Scope includes removal of ADA barriers.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-012: Rehabilitation of existing campus pedestrian and vehicle infrastructure, including concrete and asphalt paving, sidewalks, drainage structures, pedestrian and vehicle controls (signage, gates, bollards, and striping).
measureWProjectScopes.DS-013: Develop and implement a comprehensive storm water management plan for Mission College and West Valley College to improve drainage, reduce flooding risk, and support regulatory compliance.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-014: Upgrade of security and access controls at various buildings identified in the security master plan on West Valley and Mission College campuses. Scope includes door hardware replacement, electronic access control upgrades, alarm monitoring system upgrades, and security camera modernization.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-015: Upgrade fire alarm systems across Mission College and West Valley College to improve life safety, reliability, and code compliance. Scope includes fire alarm system assessment.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-016: Assess and implement HVAC and lighting energy retrofit projects across Mission College and West Valley College to improve energy efficiency, system performance, and occupant comfort.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-017: District-wide restoration and replacement of existing roof systems at Mission College's Gillmor Center and IS Building, and West Valley's Campus Center, Gym Buildings and PE Buildings. Scope includes removal, replacement and restoration of existing roof waterproofing systems including, but not limited to membrane, flashings, substrate, and insulation.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-400000: Election and legal expenses, district office labor associated with the Measures C and W Bond Programs.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-435000: Contingency budget for District and District-wide projects.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-435002: Design and construct new Administration building.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-435006: Management and related costs associated with the Measures C and W Bond Programs.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-435063: Construct a single story, 9,860 sq ft Public Safety Building in the existing parking lot 1 at the West Valley Campus. Building includes office spaces, employee break room, locker rooms, secure evidence storage space and armory, conference rooms, and a large community room. Scope includes a secure vehicle yard, new parking spaces, and other site improvements.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-435066: The Vasona Creek Trail Project enhances safe, accessible pedestrian connections within West Valley College by reconstructing walkways, ramps, and parking areas and by creating new ADA-compliant trail routes. The project includes new pathways, wooden boardwalks, retaining walls, and designated overlooks to improve mobility and outdoor access.
measureWProjectScopes.DS-435069: Upgrading the data center by installing a new cooling system including in-row cooling units, condensing units, as well as new server racks and supporting infrastructure. The project includes new Uninterruptable Power Supply (UPS) equipment and associated power infrastructure to support the upgraded mechanical and electrical systems. Scope includes removal of existing chillers, perimeter cooling units, condensing units, refrigerant piping, UPS equipment, and portions of the existing server-rack layout.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420003: Contingency budget for Mission College projects.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420006: Renovation of an existing 1-story approximately 9,000 sq ft Child Development Center building. Scope includes construction of childcare labs, student classrooms, staff offices, food prep area, exterior play yards, and shade structures.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420007: Construction of a 3-story, approximately 55,000 sq ft building housing lab classrooms, lab prep spaces, student organization spaces, and a multi-use space for campus activities as part of the overall campus STEM complex. Scope includes demo of existing structures and improvement of adjacent hard- and soft-scape spaces.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420008: Project provides for modernization of existing PE facilities and infrastructure as well as adding a multipurpose field and support facilities. Scope also includes upgrading athletic fields for artificial turf.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420009: Renovation of an existing 1-story 30,000 sq ft Campus Library. Scope includes demolition and replacing of existing finishes and infrastructure, and construction of an exterior concrete patio including seating and shade structures.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420010: Relocate recycling, waste, and storage facilities from parking lot D to be adjacent the Facilities building.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420012: Renovation of an existing 2-story 35,000 sq ft Campus Center Building. Scope includes demolition and replacement of existing finishes and infrastructure, modification of existing kitchen ventilation, and fire protection systems.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420013: Renovation of an existing 2-story, approximately 33,000 sq ft building housing lab classrooms, lab prep spaces, student organization spaces, and staff offices as part of the overall campus STEM complex. Scope includes demo of existing finishes and infrastructure.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420014: Perform minor renovations to the TAV Building to address targeted facility improvements and maintain functionality of the space. Work may include limited interior upgrades, repairs, and system enhancements to support ongoing use.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420015: Construction of temporary facilities for use during construction of other Bond-funded projects. Scope includes construction of classrooms, offices, and other temporary instructional spaces in existing buildings. Scope may include electrical, AV, FFE, and finishes to accommodate student and instructional needs.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420016: Develop and implement a comprehensive campus-wide signage and wayfinding program in alignment with the Facilities Master Plan to improve navigation, identity, and overall user experience at Mission College. The project will establish consistent standards for exterior and interior signage, including vehicular, pedestrian, and building identification systems. Implementation will enhance campus accessibility, visibility, and intuitive circulation for students, staff, and visitors.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420017: Site improvements in alignment with the Facilities Master Plan to restore and enhance campus landscaping through targeted improvements to planting, irrigation, and site features to support a cohesive and sustainable campus environment. The project includes the addition of defined campus gateway elements at key entry points to strengthen identity, visibility, and arrival experience.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420019: Targeted upgrades in alignment with the Facilities Master Plan to general education facilities across multiple campus buildings, to improve functionality, flexibility, and support for evolving instructional needs. Work may include renovations to classrooms, labs, and support spaces, along with modernization of building systems and technology infrastructure.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420165: Construction of a 2-story, approximately 38,000 sq ft building to replace the existing Mission Temporaries classroom portables. Scope includes construction of classrooms, lab space, and offices.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420166: Construction of a 2-story, 32,000 sq ft Performing Arts building. Scope includes ground improvements; construction of a terraced auditorium, stage, and back of house support spaces; additional scope includes construction of classrooms, practice rooms, offices, exterior improvements and pre-function spaces.
measureWProjectScopes.MC-420168: The Technology System Refresh projects support the District's ongoing program to replace aging instructional and operational technology that has reached or exceeded its lifecycle. The scope includes the phased replacement of classroom audio-visual systems, instructional and lab computers, and related technology infrastructure across both colleges. These upgrades ensure classrooms remain reliable, compatible with modern software and devices, and capable of supporting current instructional needs. The program prioritizes the oldest systems first to reduce equipment failures, improve security, and maintain consistent technology standards across campuses.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415000: Contingency budget for West Valley College projects.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415010: This project provides for the construction of a new Performing Arts Center at West Valley College, comprised of a new music building and fully renovated theater building. The new music building will include restrooms, practice rooms, performance space, and support spaces. Scope will include demolition of the old music building. The theater will be fully renovated, including seismic and structural upgrades and an expansion to the basement to increase the size of the wardrobe and makeup departments. The renovation also includes performance lighting and audiovisual systems, as well as the construction of a new black box theater. Scope includes demolition of the drama lab.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415011: Construction of a new black box theater and renovation to the existing theater building, including seismic and structural upgrades. Scope includes demolition of the existing drama lab.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415013: Construct a new Wellness Center to support the athletics programs on campus and community spaces for public use. The new 99,000 sq ft building will provide a gymnasium and locker rooms, nutrition center, sports medicine and rehabilitation room, and an athlete training facility. The second floor of the building will provide offices, technology classrooms, and a tutoring center to support student athlete academics.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415015: Upgrade air-handling equipment, installing modern heating and cooling units, and upgrade fume hoods in the Science and Math Building to support laboratories, preparation areas, and nearby offices.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415016: Construct a new baseball complex comprised of a team room with lockers, upgraded batting cages and pitching labs, a public gathering space and restrooms for spectators, a press box, offices, new stadium seating and dugouts, and a new turf field.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415017: Installation of new Gateway Monument signs at each entrance to the West Valley Campus for improved campus navigation.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415018: The modular classrooms block known as the villages located in lot 2 have served as swing space for the capital improvement projects at the West Valley College campus for the Measures C and W Bond Programs. Upon completion of Measure W, the villages will be removed and the parking lot will be restored.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415020: Renovating the existing West Valley College Library to create a modern Learning Resource Commons that brings together tutoring services, technology resources, study spaces, and student support programs in one updated facility. The work will include reconfiguring interior spaces, improving accessibility, and adding specialized areas such as study rooms, digital media and production studios, a maker space, and a cafe. The project will also upgrade major building systems including structural, mechanical, electrical, fire protection, roofing, and lighting to meet current safety, energy, and academic standards. These improvements will enhance natural lighting, safety, and long-term functionality to better support daily student use and evolving campus needs.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415021: The project will demolish the existing Guirlani House and prepare the site for future work after demolition has been completed.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415022: Installation of new vehicular wayfinding signage along the perimeter road to improve navigation and campus safety.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415024: Upgrade the Administration building's security systems and renovate the existing main restrooms to improve safety, reliability, and user experience. Security improvements include modernizing the card-access system and integrating updated video surveillance equipment into the district's network to provide a fully functional and compliant system. Restroom work includes installation of new finishes, fixtures, ceilings, lighting, and ventilation components.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415025: The project constructs a new Child Development Center to improve the amenities, safety, and security of the child occupants and supporting staff. The previous Child Development Center will be removed as part of this project.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415027: Site improvements to existing campus pathways and pedestrian bridges to improve circulation, safety, and overall campus connectivity, aligning with the Facilities Master Plan's focus on accessibility, safety, and creating a more cohesive and inviting campus environment. Scope will include an assessment to identify needed repairs, upgrades, and accessibility improvements.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415030: Construction of a 2-story, approximately 19,000 sq ft Fine Arts Building. Scope includes art studios, gallery space, machine shop, offices, exterior courtyard, and exterior work areas.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415100: The Technology System Refresh projects support the District's ongoing program to replace aging instructional and operational technology that has reached or exceeded its lifecycle. The scope includes the phased replacement of classroom audio-visual systems, instructional and lab computers, and related technology infrastructure across both colleges. These upgrades ensure classrooms remain reliable, compatible with modern software and devices, and capable of supporting current instructional needs. The program prioritizes the oldest systems first to reduce equipment failures, improve security, and maintain consistent technology standards across campuses.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415120: The project provides temporary instruction space for staff and students while construction projects are in progress.
measureWProjectScopes.WV-415140: Addition of a 1-story, approximately 3,600 sq ft Planetarium to the existing Science and Math Building. Scope includes planetarium space, an exhibit area, and support spaces.

notes: Expenditures are paid and accrued expenses through the reporting-period end date.
Encumbrances are committed amounts not yet reported as expenditures.
The combined program includes Measure C and Measure W bond proceeds plus state and other co-funding.
Figures are transcribed from a district draft report and may include rounding differences.
